title: sections CRUD tRPC endpoints (create, reorder, rename, delete)
description: Implement `sections.list`, `sections.create`, `sections.rename`, `sections.reorder`, `sections.delete` tRPC procedures, accessible to both `admin` and `editor` roles per the confirmed permission decision (TASK-24).
acceptanceCriteria: `sections.list` (protectedProcedure query) returns all sections ordered by `ordem`
`sections.create` (protectedProcedure mutation) accepts `{ titulo }`, assigns the next `ordem` value, and returns the created section
`sections.rename` (protectedProcedure mutation) accepts `{ id, titulo }` and updates the title
`sections.reorder` (protectedProcedure mutation) accepts an ordered list of section ids and updates `ordem` for all of them atomically
`sections.delete` (protectedProcedure mutation) accepts `{ id }`, and cascades or blocks deletion depending on whether the section has pages (document and implement the chosen behavior)
All procedures reject unauthenticated callers with `UNAUTHORIZED`; both `admin` and `editor` roles succeed

steps.description: Create sections router with list and create

steps.details: Add `rename: protectedProcedure.input(z.object({ id: z.string(), titulo: z.string().min(1) })).mutation(...)`.
Add `reorder: protectedProcedure.input(z.object({ orderedIds: z.array(z.string()) })).mutation(...)` that, in a single transaction, sets `ordem` for each id to its index in the array.

Add `delete: protectedProcedure.input(z.object({ id: z.string() })).mutation(...)`. If the section has child `pages`, either cascade-delete (and their tabs/blocks) or throw a `BAD_REQUEST` requiring the pages be removed first — pick cascade delete for MVP simplicity and document it.

steps.description: Merge sections router into root router
Test all procedures as both roles
steps.details: Test create/rename/reorder/delete as `editor` and confirm success (per confirmed decision); test as unauthenticated caller and confirm `UNAUTHORIZED`; test reorder with a partial/invalid id list and confirm a clear validation error.

technicalNotes: Cascade-deleting a section removes all nested pages/tabs/blocks/revisions — surface a confirmation step in the UI (TASK-23) before calling delete, since this is destructive and irreversible in the MVP (no soft-delete/trash).
